replies.msg:
CRUCIAL NOTE: This is of particular importance. As per the official Lega Serie A website, the club trained players currently in our squad are Claudio Marchisio, Paolo De Ceglie and Daniele Rugani. This means that there is a difference between the UEFA and Lega Serie A rules regarding player registration. Rugani spent the 2012-13 season as a part of our youth squad, and then he was co-owned by the two clubs, but played for Empoli from July 2013 to January 2015. We signed him outright in January 2015, and kept him at Empoli on loan until the summer of 2015. I am not sure if the 2015-16 season he spent here in Turin counts towards his club trained status, as his birthday is in July, before the official season begins. There is hence a massive question mark here regarding the threshold for your 21st year and being considered club trained, and the registration of the player. Rugani is not considered club trained for UEFA competitions.

Sturaro hasn't decided’
By Football Italia staff

Stefano Sturaro’s agent confirms Juventus have received offers from West Ham United and Leicester City but “he doesn’t have a preferred destination”.

It appears the Italian international will leave Turin this summer, but a deal isn’t expected to be completed in the coming days.

“In reality there’s not the air of an imminent sale,” Carlo Volpi told JuveNews.eu.

“There are several offers on Juve’s table to evaluate. Anything can happen in football, because there are in fact many offers.

“There’s not only the big clubs West Ham and Leicester, but at least four other clubs interested in my client, who doesn’t have a preferred destination.

“We’re just waiting for a decision by the Bianconeri.”

Cristiano Ronaldo might be a medical marvel, after tests at his new club Juventus showed the Portuguese superstar actually has the physical capacity of a 20-year-old.

The 33-year-old has just departed former club Real Madrid after almost a decade with Los Blancos and he more than cemented his place as a genuine legend at the La Liga giants after winning four Champions League trophies during his spell at the Bernabeu.

Usually, players in their early-to-mid thirties tend to slow down and dip in performances but, if anything, Ronaldo appears to be getting stronger in his old age.

According to results filtered out from the medical department at the Juve Stadium, Ronaldo has body fat percentage of just 7% and his muscle mass is an incredible 50%

According to Spanish publication AS, the results Ronaldo turned in are usually found in elite players around the age of 20 – not 33.

Here are the details from today's Gazzetta on the whole Bonucci situation that everyone has been asking about, the unreleased details that will be said in the coming hours...
Gazzetta say that since January, Bonucci started thinking about going back to Juventus, and now these past few days he's been letting it know that he's ready to return & play under Allegri again.
A few weeks ago when Milan & Juve met for Higuain, the idea of Bonucci came up & this is a swap deal that "could really happen".
Milan want €35m for Bonucci, Juventus want €60m for Higuain. They could make something work.
One of the problems could be Bonucci's salary which is €7.5m a year but goes up to €10m with bonuses included which could be an issue for Juventus. "either way Bonucci is up for returning to Juve"

No Rugani without Higuain?
By Football Italia staff

Gazzetta dello Sport reports that Juventus will only allow Daniele Rugani to join Chelsea if the Blues also make an offer for Gonzalo Higuain.

The centre-back has been heavily linked with a move to Stamford Bridge throughout this summer’s transfer window, with Maurizio Sarri a huge admirer of his ability.

It is thought that the 23-year-old has already agreed personal terms with the London club, reportedly on double the wages he currently earns at Juve.

However, the Italian daily claims that the Old Lady also want Chelsea to bid for Higuain, who looks set to leave following the arrival of Cristiano Ronaldo.

Although Chelsea have had contact with the Argentine’s agent, no agreement is in place with the player or his current club.

Higuain, who worked with Sarri while the duo were at Napoli, is also being linked with a transfer to Milan.

Juve looking to Godin?
By Football Italia staff

Juventus are keen on signing Atletico Madrid’s Uruguayan defender Diego Godin, according to reports.

The Old Lady are on the hunt for new defensive recruits, with Daniele Rugani likely to join Chelsea.

They are also being heavily linked with a reunion with Milan captain Leonardo Bonucci, who they only sold to the Rossoneri last summer – as well as Godin’s Atleti teammate Stefan Savic.

According to TMW Web, Godin fits exactly the profile of player that the Bianconeri are looking to bring in this summer – those ready to win immediately.

At 32, Godin is at the peak of his powers and has established himself as one of European football’s finest defenders during his time in the Spanish capital.

Rojiblancos boss Diego Simeone does not want to lose one of his key players, but Juve are claimed to have already offered Godin a three-year contract on wages of €6m a season.

ADL: ‘Napoli close to Arias’
By Football Italia staff

Napoli President Aurelio De Laurentiis confirms his club are close to signing Santiago Arias from PSV Eindhoven.

The Colombian full-back has been heavily linked with the Partenopei in recent weeks and it now appears a matter of time before he is confirmed as a San Paolo player.

“I think that we are now in the closing minutes. We are negotiating,” film producer De Laurentiis told Radio Kiss Kiss Napoli.

“The strange thing is that we are currently negotiating with two players at the same time who play in the same role.”

With Arias set to arrive, his fellow defender Kalidou Koulibaly has been touted for a move to some of European football’s leading clubs.

However, De Laurentiis - who reportedly rejected a €100m bid from an unnamed club yesterday - insists the Senegal international has a contract.

“He has a contract. We will talk about it, as you do between friends.”

The Napoli patron believes Serie A is set for an exciting season ahead and is close to surpassing England’s Premier League as the most interesting in Europe.

“The enthusiasm of fans is not determined just by Napoli, who have been playing in Europefor nine years and competing in the top positions.

“Everyone is improving, there is a great revolution in football. Serie A is becoming the most interesting League in Europe.

“It’s not missing much before it overtakes the English League - at least in terms of interest, still not in terms of revenue.”

The Napoli patron believes Serie A is set for an exciting season ahead and is close to surpassing England’s Premier League as the most interesting in Europe.

“The enthusiasm of fans is not determined just by Napoli, who have been playing in Europefor nine years and competing in the top positions.

“Everyone is improving, there is a great revolution in football. Serie A is becoming the most interesting League in Europe.

“It’s not missing much before it overtakes the English League - at least in terms of interest, still not in terms of revenue.”

有哂相睇到佢走去傾老師愛回家

關咩事？
先唔講上季b15 36歲都仲出得咁多，直接分薄左rugani歐戰機會
宜家講緊就算對於caldara黎講，bonucci黎都唔係一件好事
無論經驗實力，佢現時都唔夠bonucci同b4爭
bonucci黎其實真係做硬第四中堅
聽做rugani2.0
如果賣b4,咪又要搵多個翻黎
唔入bonucci 照順位排起碼可以爭下c3同b4嘅輪換
如果只係一心玩即食嘅就唔該咪撚害人啦
到真係熟哂先買啦
再者，rugani有個意甲青訓位，留黎同caldara良性競爭，我真係睇唔到有咩壞處，起碼兩個實力年齡都相約，打得起真係未來十年唔洗憂
為左25m而放棄個青訓點睇都弊多於利啦
我地有幾多幼幼而家可以用，大家心知肚明啦
我講嘅係馬總對佢嘅態度
你睇佢對 Caldara 同 Rugani 完全係兩個取態
真係咁在意個青訓位同佢嘅潛力
就唔會一早俾佢同車仔 agree personal terms
Rugani 講到想留都照想賣俾車
其實由頭到尾馬總都無特別表態過想賣
我覺得佢一直都猶豫緊，而車仔就不停加價
vidal咁重要，40m都即放
而家個後備俾人bid到45m,依佢平時性格一早放左
車仔又認左有出bid,但吹左成個月都得個吹字
就知馬總真係猶豫緊賣唔賣啦
btw,剛剛rugani經理人認左車仔有出bid,但現階段球員同球會都無意賣走

Davide Torchia: "The interest from Chelsea is there for Rugani. Beyond the figures though there’s a technical question, neither the player nor Juventus have ever said they want a sale. The operation with Chelsea is separate from Higuain, each deal has a different cost."

馬總照追
對 Rugani 咁有信心點解唔直接留佢

Chelsea still want Higuain?
By Football Italia staff

Gazzetta dello Sport reports Chelsea could come back into contention for the signing of Gonzalo Higuain, should negotiations continue to stall with Milan.

Yesterday, the Argentine forward appeared certain to complete a move to San Siro after Juventus and the Rossoneri agreed a deal, which also includes the exchange of Mattia Caldara and Leonardo Bonucci.

But it has emerged that Higuain is unhappy with the idea of moving on an initial loan deal – something that Milan seemingly have to do in order to satisfy Financial Fair Play regulations.

He also wants the Diavolo to pay him wages of €9m-a-season.

Negotiations will continue today - with Milan remaining favourites to sign Higuain - but Gazzetta reports Chelsea could be back in the running for the 30-year-old’s signature.

According to the Italian daily, the Blues are in advanced negotiations to sell forward Olivier Giroud to French club Marseille.

Once that deal is completed, the Stamford Bridge club will reportedly have the finances to make an offer to Juve to match that of Milan’s.

New Chelsea boss Maurizio Sarri has not given up hope of a reunion with Higuain, with the duo having worked together at Napoli.

And the English Premier League club should be able to satisfy both Higuain’s wage demands and his desire to complete a permanent transfer away from Turin.

Juventus have made a move to bolster their second string, signing Germany youth international Idrissa Toure from Werder Bremen on loan.

Werder Bremen have confirmed midfielder Idrissa Toure has joined Juventus on loan until the end of the season, with the Serie A champions having the option to make the move permanent in 2019.

Toure, 20, has spent just a year at Werder having previously been at RB Leipzig and then Schalke, featuring for both clubs' reserve sides.

The Berlin-born midfielder has just a single first-team appearance to his name in his fledgling career, after making a brief cameo for Leipzig in the second tier during the 2015-16 campaign.

Toure will initially join up with Juve's reserve team in Serie C, but should the former Germany Under-19 international impress in Italy, the Old Lady can take up their purchase option before his season-long loan expires.

http://www.goal.com/en/news/juventus-take-exciting-youngster-toure-on-loan-from-werder/w3835hylnv371m4c06qk3eoim

According to Tuttosport, Paul Pogba is likely to leave Manchester United in this transfer window due to his growing problems with Coach Jose Mourinho.

The Bainconeri still have a couple of weeks before the end of the transfer market and a return for the World Cup winner would complete their insane transfers in this window.

The source suggest that Juventus could swap former Roma midfielder Miralem Pjanic with the Frenchman to be able to land Pogba back in Turin.

Mourinho is a huge fan of Pjanic and it is believed that Juventus could offer the player to the Red Devils if it means getting the Octopus back to the Allianz Stadium.

Pogba and Pjanic never crossed paths with the Old Lady, as the Bosnian was signed by Juventus right after Pogba was sold to Manchester United in the summer of 2016.

https://m.calciomercato.com/en/news/transfer-news-juventus-prepare-mega-swap-deal-to-sign-pogba-34699

Sporting want Sturaro
By Football Italia staff

Reports in both Portugal and Italy state that Sporting CP are keen to sign Juventus midfielder Stefano Sturaro.

The Italian international has been linked with an exit all summer, but so far no club has been willing to meet the €20m asking price.

Fiorentina, Leicester City and Genoa have all been linked with Sturaro, but both Record and Sky Italia report that Sporting have now entered the picture.

Sturaro made just seven Serie A starts last season, as well as making five appearances in the Champions League.
